differentials.clue: The pupil is widely dilated with absent light and near responses; failure to constrict to pilocarpine supports pharmacologic blockade.

differentials.clue: An ulcer combines an epithelial defect with a stromal infiltrate, pain, photophobia, and anterior-chamber inflammation; it needs urgent treatment.

differentials.name: dendritic ulcer
differentials.clue: Branching fluorescein stain with terminal bulbs; avoid topical steroids.

differentials.name: retained foreign body
differentials.clue: Evert the upper lid and inspect the cornea and conjunctiva; a subtarsal foreign body may produce repeated vertical epithelial scratches.

differentials.name: positive Seidel test
differentials.clue: Aqueous leakage creates a dark streaming track through fluorescein, indicating a full-thickness corneal or scleral wound.

source: Thyroglossal Cyst - Surgical Skill
summary: Midline neck swelling moving with tongue protrusion.
identify: Midline neck swelling moving with tongue protrusion.
examAngle: Differentiate from thyroid swelling, dermoid cyst, lymph node.
quickRevision: At 00:12-00:28, verify: thyroglossal duct cyst, hyoid bone relation, Sistrunk operation. Closest differentials: thyroid swelling, dermoid cyst, lymph node.

differentials.name: thyroid swelling
differentials.clue: Moves with swallowing but not tongue protrusion; thyroid function/ultrasound.

differentials.name: dermoid cyst
differentials.clue: Midline subcutaneous lesion that usually does not move with tongue protrusion.

differentials.name: lymph node
differentials.clue: Separate from midline tract/thyroid; assess mobility, tenderness, and drainage area.

title: Hernia cough impulse
subject: Surgery
source: Visible cough impulse in a patient of ventral hernia - Dr. Md.Ibrahim Khalil Shahin
summary: Expansile cough impulse demonstration.
identify: Expansile cough impulse demonstration.
examAngle: Differentiate from hydrocele, lymph node, saphena varix, femoral hernia.
quickRevision: At 00:00-00:05, verify: reducible hernia, expansile cough impulse, direct vs indirect hernia. Closest differentials: hydrocele, lymph node, saphena varix, femoral hernia.

differentials.name: hydrocele
differentials.clue: Transilluminates and cannot get above swelling; cough impulse absent.

differentials.name: lymph node
differentials.clue: Separate from midline tract/thyroid; assess mobility, tenderness, and drainage area.

differentials.name: saphena varix
differentials.clue: Bluish groin swelling with cough impulse; disappears lying down, venous hum possible.

differentials.name: femoral hernia
differentials.clue: Below and lateral to pubic tubercle; higher strangulation risk.

title: Trendelenburg sign for hip abductor weakness
subject: Orthopedics / Neurology
source: Trendelenburg's Test - OSCE Guide Clip - Geeky Medics
summary: Direct standing Trendelenburg test showing pelvic drop from hip abductor weakness.
identify: Direct standing Trendelenburg test showing pelvic drop from hip abductor weakness.
examAngle: Differentiate from Trendelenburg gait, antalgic gait, coxa vara, hip dislocation.
quickRevision: At 00:10-00:28, verify: gluteus medius and minimus, superior gluteal nerve, stance leg. Closest differentials: Trendelenburg gait, antalgic gait, coxa vara, hip dislocation.

differentials.name: Trendelenburg gait
differentials.clue: Single-leg stance shows pelvic drop on the unsupported side; test hip abductors.

differentials.name: antalgic gait
differentials.clue: Pain location decides it; examine hip, knee, ankle, spine, and weight-bearing X-ray when needed.

differentials.name: coxa vara
differentials.clue: Reduced femoral neck-shaft angle can shorten the limb and weaken the abductor lever arm; confirm on pelvis X-ray.

differentials.name: hip dislocation
differentials.clue: Assess limb length, hip motion, and instability; radiography distinguishes dislocation from isolated abductor weakness.

differentials.name: L5 radiculopathy
differentials.clue: Check hip abduction, ankle dorsiflexion, great-toe extension, sensory change, reflexes, and radicular pain.

differentials.name: superior gluteal neuropathy
differentials.clue: Isolated gluteus medius/minimus weakness causes pelvic drop without the broader L5 motor-sensory pattern.

title: Buerger test
subject: Surgery / Vascular
source: Buerger's Test - Peripheral Artery Disease - Med Education
summary: Peripheral arterial disease bedside test.
identify: Peripheral arterial disease bedside test.
examAngle: Differentiate from venous insufficiency, cellulitis, neuropathic foot.
quickRevision: At 00:11-00:34, verify: elevation pallor, dependent rubor, Buerger angle. Closest differentials: venous insufficiency, cellulitis, neuropathic foot.

differentials.name: venous insufficiency
differentials.clue: Edema, pigmentation, ulcers near medial malleolus; duplex venous scan.

differentials.name: cellulitis
differentials.clue: Hot tender erythematous skin with systemic inflammation.

differentials.name: neuropathic foot
differentials.clue: Loss of protective sensation, ulcers at pressure points, pulses may be present.

title: Simple interrupted suturing
subject: Surgery
source: Simple interrupted suture - OSCE Guide - Geeky Medics
summary: Short simple interrupted suture demonstration.
identify: Short simple interrupted suture demonstration.
examAngle: Irrigate, anesthetize, approximate without strangulation, and provide tetanus/antibiotics when indicated.
management: Irrigate, anesthetize, approximate without strangulation, and provide tetanus/antibiotics when indicated.
quickRevision: At 00:26-00:55, verify: wound closure, needle angle, knot placement. Closest differentials: mattress suture, subcuticular suture, continuous suture.

differentials.name: mattress suture
differentials.clue: Everting tension-relieving stitch; different bite pattern from simple interrupted.

differentials.name: subcuticular suture
differentials.clue: Intradermal cosmetic closure, no external interrupted knots.

differentials.name: continuous suture
differentials.clue: One running strand; faster but failure can affect whole closure.

title: Closed gowning and gloving
subject: Surgery
source: Gowning and gloving - AMBOSS tutorial - AMBOSS: Medical Knowledge Distilled
summary: Direct sterile gowning followed by the closed-gloving technique.
identify: Direct sterile gowning followed by the closed-gloving technique.
examAngle: Differentiate from open gloving, assisted gloving, sterile-field contamination.
quickRevision: At 02:10-02:55, verify: surgical asepsis, hands remain inside gown cuffs, glove cuff covers gown cuff. Closest differentials: open gloving, assisted gloving, sterile-field contamination.

differentials.name: open gloving
differentials.clue: Used when the operator is not yet gowned; fingers touch only the folded glove cuff.

differentials.name: assisted gloving
differentials.clue: A scrubbed team member may help a gowned operator don gloves while preserving cuff sterility; this differs from self closed-gloving.

differentials.name: sterile-field contamination
differentials.clue: Any hand drop below waist, non-sterile contact, or wet strike-through contaminates the field; restart or reglove.

differentials.name: lie vs presentation vs position vs attitude

title: McRoberts maneuver for shoulder dystocia
subject: OBGY / Emergency
source: SimMom ADM Shoulder Dystocia - Laerdal Medical
summary: Simulation video of McRoberts maneuver.
identify: Simulation video of McRoberts maneuver.
examAngle: Differentiate from fundal pressure, Woods screw, Rubin maneuver.
quickRevision: At 01:08-01:38, verify: shoulder dystocia, first-line maneuver, suprapubic pressure. Closest differentials: fundal pressure, Woods screw, Rubin maneuver.

differentials.name: fundal pressure
differentials.clue: Avoid in shoulder dystocia; worsens impaction and increases uterine rupture risk.

differentials.name: Woods screw
differentials.clue: Rotational maneuver after first-line McRoberts/suprapubic pressure if needed.

differentials.name: Rubin maneuver
differentials.clue: Adducts fetal shoulder by pressure on posterior aspect; rotational rescue step.

title: Bimanual uterine compression / uterine massage for PPH
subject: OBGY / Emergency
source: BEMONC Bimanual Uterine Compression demo - Jennifer Araneta
summary: Simulation/mannequin demonstration.
identify: Simulation/mannequin demonstration.
examAngle: Atonic PPH needs uterine massage, oxytocin, bimanual compression, escalation, and resuscitation.
management: Atonic PPH needs uterine massage, oxytocin, bimanual compression, escalation, and resuscitation.
quickRevision: At 00:00-01:00, verify: atonic PPH, uterine massage, bimanual compression. Closest differentials: traumatic PPH, retained placenta, uterine inversion.

differentials.name: traumatic PPH
differentials.clue: Firm uterus with bleeding suggests laceration, rupture, or hematoma.

differentials.name: retained placenta
differentials.clue: Placenta incomplete or not delivered; examine placenta and consider manual removal.

differentials.name: uterine inversion
differentials.clue: Shock, severe pain, absent fundus, mass at introitus; replace uterus urgently.

title: Pap smear collection technique
subject: OBGY
source: Pap Smear Demonstration on Model Figure - Dr Natalia Novikova
summary: Simulation or model-based Pap smear demonstration.
identify: Simulation or model-based Pap smear demonstration.
examAngle: Sample the transformation zone and follow age-appropriate cervical-screening algorithms.
management: Sample the transformation zone and follow age-appropriate cervical-screening algorithms.
quickRevision: At 00:08-00:54, verify: transformation zone, Ayre spatula, cytobrush. Closest differentials: VIA, HPV testing, colposcopy-guided biopsy.

differentials.name: VIA
differentials.clue: Acetic acid visual inspection; not cytology sampling from transformation zone.

differentials.name: HPV testing
differentials.clue: Molecular high-risk HPV test; sample handling differs from Pap cytology.

differentials.name: colposcopy-guided biopsy
differentials.clue: Directed tissue biopsy after abnormal screen, not screening collection itself.

title: IUCD insertion technique

management: Exclude pregnancy and infection risk; counsel about perforation, expulsion, bleeding, and strings.
quickRevision: At 01:48-02:30, verify: timing, contraindications, complications. Closest differentials: emergency contraception, postpartum IUCD, interval IUCD.

differentials.name: emergency contraception
differentials.clue: Post-coital contraception timing; copper IUCD can be used within guideline window.

differentials.name: postpartum IUCD
differentials.clue: Insertion timing is immediately postplacental or delayed; expulsion counseling matters.

differentials.name: interval IUCD
differentials.clue: Inserted when not pregnant outside postpartum/postabortal setting.

title: Neonatal resuscitation initial steps
subject: Pediatrics / Emergency
source: Cord Management, Initial Steps, & Ventilation, Neonatal Resuscitation Education Video 1, 2023 - TexSun NeoEd
summary: Mannequin/simulation video of initial NRP steps.
identify: Mannequin/simulation video of initial NRP steps.
examAngle: Warm, dry, stimulate, position airway, assess breathing and heart rate, then provide PPV if needed.
management: Warm, dry, stimulate, position airway, assess breathing and heart rate, then provide PPV if needed.
quickRevision: At 02:07-02:47, verify: warm, dry, stimulate. Closest differentials: when to start chest compressions, oxygen use, delayed cord clamping.

differentials.name: when to start chest compressions
differentials.clue: In NRP, start compressions if HR remains below 60/min after effective PPV.

differentials.name: oxygen use
differentials.clue: Use gestation-appropriate oxygen and pulse oximetry targets; avoid routine 100 percent oxygen.

differentials.name: delayed cord clamping
differentials.clue: Delay only if newborn is vigorous and resuscitation is not immediately required.
